Skip to main workspace
Compliance advisor workshop tool

CMMC L2 Gap Workshop Tool v76

Use this after kickoff and initial document intake to prepare for, facilitate, and document a client workshop. The goal is to clarify compliance responsibility, evidence ownership, documentation gaps, provider dependencies, and workbook handoff items before detailed objective-level Gap Analysis Workbook review begins.

Work lens
Saved locally
Advisor workflowClient workshop flow Use these six steps for the advisor workflow. Open the Dashboard anytime for a status overview. Use this clean path when screen-sharing: purpose, ownership, key practices, evidence follow-up, and summary.
Additional toolsUse the six-step workflow above for normal operation. Open More tools for supplementary reports, registers, imports, technical checks, or direct page access.Client Mode keeps the clean client/joint flow. Advisor-only imports, workbook controls, technical checks, and source registers remain hidden.

Client validation session start Client-facing

Use this page to open the client workshop. It sets a clear purpose before reviewing ownership, evidence needs, and next steps. The goal is to validate readiness assumptions and agree on follow-up, not to collect controlled evidence or make final assessment determinations.

Important: this is a readiness and responsibility validation session, not a final CMMC assessment decision. Do not paste CUI, credentials, sensitive screenshots, export files, or controlled technical data into this tool, meeting chat, or email follow-up threads.

1. What we will validate

We will confirm scope assumptions, major capability ownership, provider dependencies, and which evidence items are needed before detailed workbook review.

2. What we will not do

We will not assign a final CMMC score, make assessor determinations, or collect controlled evidence in the meeting chat or email body.

3. How follow-up works

We will leave with a short evidence follow-up email and a tracker. When useful, the advisor may also provide a pre-engagement request packet before the session. Detailed evidence should be uploaded to the approved repository or secure delivery path.

Client-facing flow

This is the path the client should see during screen-share. Advisor-only preparation, AI import, source material, and workbook handoff stay behind the scenes.

1

Start

Confirm today’s purpose, guardrails, no-CUI handling, and what will be validated together.

2

Prework

Use the pre-engagement request packet when starter ownership or evidence information would help before the live session.

3

Ownership

Validate who leads major compliance capabilities and where provider participation or evidence is needed.

4

Key practices

Review only practices where ownership, evidence, confidence, or provider dependency needs clarification.

5

Follow-up

Agree on the short list of critical documents/artifacts and how evidence should be delivered securely.

6

Summary

Confirm open decisions, next actions, expected owners, and what will move into detailed workbook review.

Pre-engagement request package Client prework

Use this page when the client can provide starter information before the live workshop. The goal is to make the first session easier: confirm major capability owners, collect a simple evidence submission tracker, and avoid sending uncontrolled technical details by email.

Client-friendly rule: use the approved repository or secure delivery path for actual evidence. Do not place CUI, credentials, sensitive screenshots, export files, or controlled technical data in email, chat, or this tool.
Preview pre-engagement email

Review this before copying into Outlook or Teams. The tool never sends email automatically. Attach or link the pre-engagement packet and tracker through approved channels.


          

        

        

          
Technology, service, and provider profile

          
The capability questionnaire now asks for the exact platform, environment or tenant tier, enabled modules, managed-service operating model, and source documents that define responsibility. This creates written input for future Azure, AWS, Microsoft 365, security-tool, SaaS, and MSP responsibility overlays.

          

            
Be specificRecord the edition, tenant, region or government/commercial environment, and enabled modules—not just the vendor name.

            
Separate capability from operationIdentify who supplies the technology, who configures it, who monitors it, and who retains assessment evidence.

            
Document the basisReference the contract, SOW, SLA, responsibility matrix, RACI, portal, export, ticket system, or other written source.

          

          

        

        

          
Prework flow

          

            
1. Ownership questionnaireIdentify the lead/coordinating owner for each major capability area before the workshop.

            
2. Starter evidence listUse the tracker for priority evidence requests, submission links, filenames, tickets, and clarifying notes.

            
3. Live validationReturned prework stays draft until the advisor reviews it during the capability or practice workshop.

          

        

        

          
What to send before the workshop

          

            

              
Prework itemBest useClient expectationAdvisor handling

              
                
Pre-Engagement Request PacketOne simple Word-compatible package for kickoff follow-up.Review instructions, answer ownership questions, and use approved channels for evidence.Use returned content as draft input only; validate before importing or changing workshop status.

                
Client Evidence TrackerPrimary working list for requested evidence.Fill in Evidence Submission with repository links, filenames, or ticket references.Use tracker responses to update evidence status only after review.

                
Capability & Technology QuestionnaireOwnership plus structured service/provider prework.Identify owners, exact service tiers/modules, operating model, responsibility sources, and provider dependencies.Re-ingest through AI/CSV only after review and preview/diff.

                
Filtered Practice PreworkOptional targeted practice questions.Use only if the advisor sends a focused subset; do not complete all 110 practices unless requested.Keep as advanced/targeted prework, not the default client ask.

              
            

          

        

      

    


        

      

        

          
Advisor help & SOP Advisor-only

          
This is the advisor reference page, not the normal starting page. Start day-to-day work on Prepare. Use this page when another advisor needs the operating model, page guide, Quick Start, SOP, or reminders about AI prefill and evidence handling.

          

            
1
Prepare (advisor)
Enter setup, document register, assumptions, and caveats from the initial document package.

            
2
Import & Review (advisor)
Use CSV/JSON import only as draft prefill. Preview diffs, reject bad rows, and apply only reviewed changes.

            
3
Validate (joint)
Review capability ownership and provider dependencies before going practice-by-practice.

            
4
Workshop (joint)
Validate practice ownership, evidence status, confidence, and notes only where clarification is needed.

            
5
Evidence Package (client)
Generate the short email and client evidence tracker; send only approved follow-up requests.

            
6
Handoff (advisor)
Refresh the handoff, export JSON/CSV, and move detailed review into the workbooks.

          

        


        

          
Detail page guide

          

          

            
PageWhen to use itWhat to do on the pageWhat to do next

            
              
DashboardAny time the advisor needs an overview.Review ownership clarity, evidence progress, provider-dependency flags, high-impact unknowns, and workbook readiness.Return to the active workflow page.

              
PrepareAfter kickoff and document intake, before client screen-share.Enter session labels and caveats. Use the built-in links to populate the Document Register and Assumptions & Decisions pages when needed.Move to Import & Review, Pre-Engagement, or Validate.

              
Pre-EngagementBefore the live workshop when client starter data would reduce meeting time.Download the pre-engagement packet, client evidence tracker, or ownership questionnaire. Keep returned content as draft until reviewed.Use returned information to support Import & Review, Validate, or Workshop discussion.

              
Import & ReviewOptional advisor-only prefill step.Paste or upload AI/CSV/JSON draft data, preview diffs, reject bad rows, and apply only checked/reviewed changes.Move to Validate once imports are staged and merged.

              
ValidateAt the beginning of the joint validation workshop.Discuss high-level capability ownership, provider dependency, and evidence maturity before practice-by-practice detail.Move to Workshop for practices tied to unclear capabilities.

              
WorkshopDuring the main joint discussion.For relevant practices, set implementation owner, evidence owner, readiness, evidence status, confidence, and notes.Use filters to focus on unknowns, provider dependencies, and requested evidence.

              
Evidence PackageNear workshop closeout or after advisor cleanup.Generate the short email and client evidence tracker. Keep the email brief and direct users to the tracker/repository for details.Mark package items requested after the request is sent.

              
Client SummaryClient readout.Show open assumptions, evidence requests, provider-dependency flags, imported workbook findings, and next actions.Use it to validate facts and close the workshop.

              
HandoffInternal post-workshop handoff.Refresh the concise summary of caveats, unknowns, gaps, evidence requests, and workbook-ready rows.Export JSON/CSV/Markdown and move detailed review into the workbooks.

              
Advanced support pagesAdvisor-only as needed.Document Register, Assumptions & Decisions, Workbook Merge, Sources, and Advisor Help provide supporting context and exports.Hide Advanced again before screen-sharing.

                        
          

          

        


        

          
Advisor Quick Start & lightweight SOP

          
Use these downloads when another advisor needs to run the same workflow. The quick start explains the order of operations; the SOP defines how to handle AI prefill, evidence sufficiency, no-CUI handling, client follow-up, and workbook handoff.

          

            
            
          

          

            
Before the workshop
Import or enter the document register, capture caveats, and mark AI/document assumptions as low-confidence until validated.

            
During the workshop
Validate scope, provider roles, capability ownership, and high-impact unknowns before trying to discuss every practice row.

            
After the workshop
Export state JSON, evidence requests, workshop change log, and handoff summary; then continue detailed review in the Gap Analysis Workbook and Frequency Matrix.

          

        


        

          
What this tool is

          
A compliance advisor workshop review layer. It helps organize responsibility, evidence ownership, document gaps, assumptions, and handoff items before formal objective-level review.

          
    
            
  • Best used with partial documentation and uncertain provider responsibilities.
    • 
            
  • Works well when a prior MSP, CSP, ESP, or tool owner may have evidence the client does not control.
    • 
            
  • Produces CSV/JSON/Markdown exports that can feed the workbooks and can import workbook merge results back into the workshop summary layer.
    • 
          

        

        

          
What this tool is not

          
It is not a C3PAO assessment tool, not a final CMMC score, and not a substitute for objective-level evidence review. It is intentionally simpler than the workbooks so it can be used live in a workshop without creating false precision.

          
    
            
  • Use Ready for Workbook Review only after ownership, evidence status, and provider/MSP responsibility are clear enough for objective-level workbook review. It still does not mean finally MET.
    • 
            
  • Use Confidence to show how strong the advisor’s basis is.
    • 
            
  • Use Caveats when document evidence is incomplete or provider evidence is unavailable.
    • 
          

        


        

          
Advisor facilitation prompts

          

          

            
Workshop questionWhy it mattersOutput to capture

            
              
What is the intended CMMC Assessment Scope?Practice responsibility depends on the boundary, asset categories, and provider roles.Scope caveat, asset/provider dependency, ownership notes.

              
Which controls were operated by a prior or external provider?The organization may lack evidence even if the control existed operationally.Provider evidence owner, unavailable evidence notes, follow-up request.

              
Which evidence exists in final form?Assessment readiness depends on actual evidence, not verbal assertions or draft documents.Evidence status and confidence by practice.

              
Which practices are ready for detailed workbook review?Keeps this tool light while feeding the formal practice/objective workbook.“Ready for Workbook Review” only after current owner/evidence/provider assumptions are clear enough for objective-level review. Use client/provider hold statuses when facts are not ready.

            
          

          

        

      

    

    

      

        

          
Workshop operator cockpit

          
Use anytime as the workshop cockpit. This dashboard summarizes ownership clarity, evidence status, provider-dependency flags, workbook-ready rows, and next actions. It is not a CMMC score and should be treated as readiness/workshop status only.

          

            
            Use this for a short workshop readout. It hides detailed tables, AI/import mechanics, and internal evidence prompt libraries.
          

          

            
1
Ownership clarity
See where implementation or evidence ownership is still unknown.

            
2
Evidence progress
Track whether key evidence is not requested, requested, received, or reviewed.

            
3
Provider dependency
Highlight practices that may require CSP/MSP/ESP/tool-owner confirmation.

            
4
Handoff readiness
Identify what is ready to move into workbook review and what still needs validation.

          

        

        

          
Operator status
One concise view of the current phase, next workspace, trust position, and unresolved blockers.
Preparing status…

          

        

        

          
Top blockers
Prioritized working issues that are most likely to delay facilitated review or workbook progress. This is not a readiness score.

          

          

        

        

          
Resume work
Return to the highest-value next step without searching the full workspace.

          

        

        

        

          
Readiness overview by practice area

          
This view helps answer the workshop questions: who owns implementation, who owns evidence, where evidence is missing, what is provider-dependent, and what is ready for formal workbook review?

          

        

        

          
Next best actions

          

        

      

    


    

      

        

          
Session setup Advisor-only

          
Use this page to establish the engagement context before importing data or meeting with the client. These values personalize exports, evidence packages, role labels, and handoff summaries.

          
Inputs: kickoff notes, organization name, environment/enclave, provider labels, assessment target, document set, and caveats. Output: consistent role labels and a saved local workspace. Use Export Workshop JSON for the engagement record; the Save button only stores data in this browser.

          

            
            
            
            
            
            
            
            
            
            
            
            
            
            
            
            
            
            
            
          

          

            Technology & provider profile Structured inputs for future responsibility overlays
            
Purpose: capture the exact service edition, hosting boundary, enabled modules, operating model, and responsibility sources in writing. These fields support future cloud/service responsibility overlays but do not assign final responsibility or determine whether a practice is met.

            

              
              
              
              
              
              
              
              
              
              
              
            

          

          

          

            
            Save stores progress in this browser only. Export Workshop JSON for the engagement record.
            
            
            
          

          
Prepare substeps: after setup, open the supporting registers to document what was reviewed and what is still assumed. These pages stay in Advanced Pages to keep the main workflow simple.

          

            
            
            
          

        

        

          
Role labels used in dropdowns

          

          
Tip: keep “implementation owner” separate from “evidence owner.” A provider may operate a control, while the organization or advisor coordinates assessment evidence.

        

      

    



    

      

        

          
Document register Advisor pre-review

          
Use this page to record every document, export, screenshot, report, or artifact received or reviewed. The register should show what exists, what is current, what was only filename-reviewed, and what was not used in workshop review.

          
Purpose: prevent vague evidence claims. Track actual artifact names, versions/dates, review basis, status, owner, and advisory relevance. Next: use the register to create assumptions/decisions and focus the client workshop on missing or stale artifacts.

          
Client view: this page helps confirm the document set and identify missing/stale artifacts before practice-level discussion.

          

            
            
            
          

          

          

          

        

      

    


    

      

        

          
Assumptions & decisions log Advisor control record

          
Use this page to preserve the rationale behind the workshop record. Capture scope assumptions, verbal confirmations, provider dependencies, advisor caveats, and open follow-up questions.

          
Purpose: make the workshop defensible. If an owner, evidence status, or readiness note is based on incomplete documents, AI prefill, or a client statement, record that basis here. Next: validate major assumptions in the Capability Map and Practice Review Matrix, then carry unresolved items into the Handoff Summary.

          

            
            
            
            
          

          

          

          

        

      

    

    

      

        

          
Capability ownership map Ownership validation

          
Use this page to confirm who leads each major compliance capability and whether the current evidence package is strong enough to move into detailed practice review. This page is not a score, finding, or assessment conclusion.

          
Client-friendly framing: we are not trying to assign every control during this step. We are confirming who can explain each capability, who can coordinate evidence, and where provider participation or follow-up documentation is still needed.

          

            
Lead ownerWho can explain the capability and coordinate follow-up.

            
Evidence maturityWhether the available evidence is missing, requested, partial, or appears ready for detailed review.

            
Documents requestedWhether follow-up artifacts have been requested for this capability area.

            
NotesKnown assumptions, provider dependencies, caveats, or decisions to validate later.

          

          
How to use: choose the lead or coordinating owner, not every party involved. Use Shared only when one lead cannot reasonably be identified; then describe the split in Notes. If ownership or evidence remains unclear, use the Practice Review Matrix only for the related practices that need deeper validation.

          

            
            
            Use the Word questionnaire for client prework. If returned, use the prompt to convert responses into CSV/JSON and preview before applying.
          

          

          

        

      

    


    

      

        

          
Practice Review One practice at a time

          
Live facilitation: review one practice at a time, confirm ownership and evidence context, capture the client discussion, and choose the appropriate workbook-review state. The tool prepares objective-level workbook review; it does not make final Met/Not Met, evidence-sufficiency, or assessment-readiness conclusions.

          
Workshop-to-workbook loop: capture workshop context here → export l2g_workbook_handoff_v1.json → use the separate Workbook Builder/Merger to combine it with a blank/template Gap Analysis Workbook .xlsx → complete objective-level workbook review → import l2g_workbook_merge_v1.json back into this tool as workbook-derived results for client follow-up and handoff.

          

            Practice Review view
            
            
            Focused Review is designed for live client discussion. Matrix View remains available for bulk filtering, comparison, and cleanup.
          

          

            When should a row go to Workbook Review?
            

              
Ready for Workbook ReviewUse when the current implementation owner, evidence owner/status, source basis, and major provider assumptions are clear enough for objective-level review in the Gap Analysis Workbook. This is not a Met/Not Met decision.

              
Hold for Client ValidationUse when the advisor has a draft note, old evidence, or document signal, but the client still needs to confirm the current fact pattern before workbook review.

              
Hold for Provider/MSP ValidationUse when prior MSP/CSP/ESP evidence, stale NEO Systems support, shared responsibility, or tool-owner access must be confirmed before relying on the row in the workbook.

            

          

          

            
            
            
            
            
            
          

          

          

            

              Advisor tools
              Hidden in Client Mode. Use these after discussion or for internal handoff.
            

            

              What do these advisor tools do?
              

                

                  
ButtonUse it when...Effect

                  
                    
Set filtered rows to RequestedYou have sent or assigned the evidence request for the rows currently visible under the filters.Changes Evidence Status to Requested for visible rows only. It does not mean evidence was received, reviewed, sufficient, or ready for workbook review.

                    
Hold filtered for Client ValidationOld document signals, imported notes, or advisor assumptions need client confirmation.Changes Readiness to Hold - Client Validation Needed and appends a note if needed.

                    
Hold filtered for Provider/MSP ValidationPrior MSP/CSP/ESP ownership, old NEO Systems evidence, shared responsibility, or provider/tool-owner evidence needs validation.Changes Readiness to Hold - Provider/MSP Validation Needed and appends a provider-validation note.

                    
Send filtered to Workbook ReviewThe row has enough current ownership/evidence/provider context for objective-level workbook review.Changes Readiness to Ready for Workbook Review. It does not mark the practice Met, sufficient, or assessment-ready.

                    
Export Practice Matrix CSVYou need a full internal practice-level export.Exports the current Practice Review Matrix with advisor/workbook fields.

                    
Export Workbook Review Queue CSVYou only want rows marked Ready for Workbook Review.Exports the subset intended for detailed workbook validation.

                    
Export Workshop Change Log CSVYou want every row that has been touched, flagged, imported, or changed.Creates the internal handoff file for the workbook/review team.

                    
Export Filtered Practice PreworkYou want a client-friendly spreadsheet for only the currently filtered practice rows.Exports a targeted Excel XML file. It keeps Practice ID for re-import mapping but omits internal-only Section and file path data.

                    
Print / Save PDFYou need a local view of this page.Uses the browser print dialog. Use the Executive Summary PDF for client-facing summary output.

                  
                

              

            

            

              
              
              
              
              
              
            

            

              Internal matrix exports
              
These are advisor handoff/database exports. Use the client tracker, capability questionnaire, or filtered prework for client-facing follow-up.

              

                
                
                
                
                
              

            

          

          

            

            

          

        

      

    

    

      

        

          
Evidence package and follow-up tracker Client follow-up

          
Use this page after the ownership/practice workshop to create a short evidence follow-up email and client-friendly tracker. Prework evidence requests belong on the Pre-Engagement page; this page is for the post-workshop “what still needs to be provided or reviewed” list.

          
Requested Evidence means the request has already been sent or assigned. Recommended Next Requests are advisor-side suggestions based on unknown ownership, high-impact practices, low confidence, or provider-dependency review flags. The client tracker should be the detailed external working file; the email should stay short and never include CUI or sensitive evidence.

          
Client working files: generate the email, copy it into Outlook/Teams, and attach or link the Client Evidence Tracker through the approved channel. These actions do not mark evidence received, reviewed, sufficient, or ready for workbook review.

          

            
            
            
          

          

            Internal raw evidence exports
            
These are advisor/reference exports. For client follow-up, use the Client Evidence Tracker and short email. Markdown/internal packages live in Output Center.

            

              
              
              
              
            

          

        

        

          
Evidence sufficiency quick guide

          
Use this as a compliance-advisor reminder before marking evidence “Reviewed - Sufficient.” The goal is to prevent policy-only or filename-only evidence from being treated like implementation evidence.

          

            
Usually sufficient
Final-form document plus implementation artifact, screenshot/export/log/ticket, correct scope, current date/version, named owner, and enough detail to support examine/interview/test.

            
Often insufficient
Policy statement only, stale or draft document, missing scope boundary, screenshots without tenant/system context, unverified client statement, or provider-owned evidence that the client cannot produce.

            
Use validation basis
Mark whether the entry is filename-only, document-content reviewed, spreadsheet/artifact reviewed, screenshot/export reviewed, client-stated, evidence-reviewed, or workbook-validated.

          

        

        

          
Evidence follow-up email and tracker

          
Use this after the workshop to turn outstanding evidence needs into client action items. The primary outputs are a short reviewable email draft and a client evidence tracker. Detailed Markdown/internal exports are available in Output Center, not the normal evidence follow-up path.

          
Recommended use: refresh the package, review the short email draft, then copy it into Outlook/Teams and attach or link the Client Evidence Tracker as the detailed follow-up list. Priorities are advisory and based on evidence status, criticality, owner clarity, provider dependency, and readiness status.

          

            
            
            
            
            
            Use the state-changing button only after the email/tracker has actually been sent or assigned. It changes Evidence Status to Requested; it does not mean evidence was received or reviewed.
          

          

            Advanced/internal package outputs
            

              
              
            

          

          

          

        


        

          Already requested / awaiting evidence
          

          

          

        

        

          Recommended next requests / reviews
          

          

          

        

        

          Full evidence prompt library
          

          

        

      

    


    

      

        

          
Client summary & closeout Client-facing

          
Use this page at the end of the client session. It summarizes what was validated, what remains open, what evidence is needed, and what happens next without exposing AI/import/admin mechanics.

          
Use during workshop readout. Walk through scope caveats, major provider dependencies, critical evidence requests, and next actions. This is not a CMMC score and should not be presented as a final assessment result.

          

            
            
            
            
          

        

        

          

        

      

    




    

      

        

          
Executive advisory report Client-ready draft

          
Generate a dedicated report from the current Workshop record. This report is separate from the application interface and is designed for browser Print / Save as PDF, Word-compatible HTML download, or Markdown handoff.

          
Reporting rule: imported and Workshop-entered information remains advisory working context. Reviewed workbook results are labeled workbook-derived review output. The report does not create a final CMMC score, final assessment determination, or evidence-sufficiency conclusion.

          

            
            
            
            
          

          

            
            
            
            
          

          
Browser print note: turn off browser headers and footers in the print dialog to prevent the local file path, page URL, date, and page title from appearing on the PDF.

        

        

          

        

        

          

        

      

    


    

      

        

          
Deliverables center Advisor deliverables

          
Use this page to choose the right deliverable instead of hunting across individual workflow pages. The goal is to keep client outputs clean and keep raw/internal exports out of the normal client package.

          
Recommended client package: Executive Summary PDF, Client Evidence Tracker for Excel, and—when useful before a workshop—the Capability Questionnaire. Use internal CSV/JSON exports for advisor handoff, workbook merge, or backup only.

        

        

          
Client-facing outputs

          

            

              Client / Executive
              
Executive Summary PDF

              
Dedicated, print-safe advisory report for leadership and client readout. It uses a report-only layout and can incorporate reviewed-workbook output without printing the application UI.

              

            

            

              Client working file
              
Client Evidence Tracker

              
Primary client follow-up tracker. v58 consolidates duplicate requests, uses complete plain-language instructions, separates client-safe notes from internal context, and groups related practices.

              

            

            

              Prework
              
Pre-Engagement Request Packet

              
Client-friendly Word-compatible package with instructions, ownership questions, and a simple priority evidence list.

              

            

            

              Prework
              
Capability & Technology Questionnaire

              
Word-compatible questionnaire for confirming capability ownership plus exact cloud/service tiers, enabled modules, managed providers, operating model, and responsibility sources before a live workshop.

              

            

          

        

        

          
Optional targeted prework

          

            

              Targeted prework
              
Filtered Practice Prework

              
Send only selected/high-impact practices when client input is needed before a working session. If no filters are set, the export defaults to a critical starter set.

              

            

            

              Meeting artifact
              
Evidence Markdown Package

              
Clean text package for meeting notes, Teams posts, tickets, SharePoint notes, or internal handoff. Does not send anything automatically.

              

            

            

              Backup
              
Workshop JSON Backup

              
Canonical state backup for this browser session. Use before major imports, after workshops, and before clearing local data.

              

            

          

        

        

          
Internal / advanced exports

          
These are intentionally grouped away from the client-facing workflow. Use them for advisor handoff, workbook merge results, troubleshooting, or audit trail—not as default client attachments.

          

            Show internal exports
            

              
              
              
              
              
              
            

          

        

        

          
Stability / data hygiene tools

          
Use these before sending client-facing outputs or after importing AI/converter results. They do not score the environment; they only check tool health, clean local file paths, and remove repeated converter metadata from the current browser session.

          

            
            
            
          

          

        

      

    


    

      

        

          
Advisor summary & workbook handoff Advisor-only

          
Use this page to package workshop results for the Gap Analysis Workbook reviewer, create the JSON input for the separate Workbook Builder/Merger, and preserve an advisor summary for meeting notes, engagement records, and follow-up.

          
Purpose: close the workshop loop and start the formal gap-analysis loop. l2g_workbook_handoff_v1.json is the primary file for the CMMC L2 Gap Workbook Builder/Merger. This JSON file does not modify the official workbook by itself; the Builder/Merger combines it with a blank/template CMMC Level 2 Gap Analysis Workbook .xlsx. The v54 handoff includes trusted-package provenance and objective-specific working context, reviewed-source-document traceability, explicit workbook-builder targets, and advisor/report support guidance so Builder/Merger can keep compact notes in the official workbook while placing concise report findings on the L2G Advisor Review tab and long detail on the L2G Detail Register tab. Excel Review Guide (.xml) is a standalone Excel-readable guide only; it is not the official workbook and does not merge into the template by itself.

          

            
            
            
            
            
          

          

            Advanced handoff exports
            
Use these only when a reviewer needs separate files instead of the L2G Workbook Handoff JSON or Excel Review Guide.

            

              
              
              
            

          

          
Workbook merge note: this tool exports JSON and XML guide files only. To create a populated official workbook, use the separate CMMC L2 Gap Workbook Builder/Merger with a blank/template .xlsx workbook and the l2g_workbook_handoff_v1.json downloaded here.

          

        

      

    


    

      

        

          
Workbook loop: handoff, builder, and merge results Post-workshop

          
Use this page for the workbook round trip: export l2g_workbook_handoff_v1.json for the separate Gap Workbook Builder/Merger, then import l2g_workbook_merge_v1.json after workbook review. The HTML tool remains the workshop/client-summary layer; the populated workbook remains the detailed objective-level gap analysis source of truth.

          
Recommended loop: download L2G Workbook Handoff JSON (.json) here → open the separate CMMC L2 Gap Workbook Builder/Merger → upload the blank/template Gap Analysis Workbook .xlsx and this JSON → download the populated workbook .xlsx → complete objective-level review in that workbook → export l2g_workbook_merge_v1.json → import it here → refresh Client Summary, Evidence Follow-up, and Handoff outputs.

        

        

          
1. Create input for Workbook Builder

          
Recommended: download the L2G Workbook Handoff JSON, then use the separate Workbook Builder/Merger to merge it into a blank/template Gap Analysis Workbook .xlsx. This page does not modify or create the official .xlsx workbook directly.

          

            
            
          

          

            Advanced workbook loop exports
            
These are separate CSV helpers for reviewers who do not want the JSON/Workbook Builder workflow or XML review guide.

            

              
              
              
            

          

        

        

          
2. Import workbook merge results

          
Import l2g_workbook_merge_v1.json from L2G Builder/Merger after workbook review. The governed preview checks package identity, practice/objective coverage, reviewed statuses, reviewer-authored changes, conflicts, and field-level differences before applying anything. Existing Workshop context remains intact; workbook-derived output is stored as a labeled overlay.

          
          
          

            
            
          

        


        

          
3. Preview Workbook Merge before applying Workbook-derived review output

          
The preview separates parent-practice results, objective statuses, evidence/gap rows, and L2G Advisor Review edits. Generated Workshop wording is not treated as a reviewer edit merely because it appears in the workbook.

          

          
Load or paste a Workbook Merge package to preview it.

          

            
            
            
          

          

        

        

          
Workbook-derived objective results

          

          

        

        

          
Workbook-derived Advisor Review decisions

          

          

        

        

          
Accepted workbook merge fields

          
FieldPurposeCommon accepted header names

            
PracticeMaps the row to a CMMC practice.Practice, Practice_ID, Control, Requirement_ID

            
Workbook_StatusDetailed workbook status or finding category.Workbook_Status, Gap Workbook Status, Assessment Status

            
Workbook_ValidationObjective-level validation stage.Workbook_Validation, Objective Review Status, Validation

            
Objective_GapsObjective-level gaps/findings to carry forward.Objective_Gaps, Gap Summary, Findings

            
Evidence_ReviewedEvidence reviewed in workbook/detail review.Evidence_Reviewed, Evidence Summary, Evidence Notes

            
Frequency_Cadence / Evidence_WindowCadence and evidence-window notes from the frequency matrix.Suggested_Cadence, Frequency_Cadence, Evidence_Window

            
POAM_StatusPOA&M eligibility/closure or blocker note.POAM_Status, POA&M, POAM_Eligibility

            
Assessor_Questions / SSP_LanguageQuestion prompts and SSP cues.Assessor_Questions, Suggested Questions, SSP_Language

          

        

        

          
Workbook merge results dashboard

          

          

        

      

    



    

      

        

          
Meeting & Workshop Context Advisor-controlled

          
Import a DocConverter-L2G meeting-context package or locally parse a Microsoft Teams .vtt, plain-text transcript, Markdown notes, or JSON transcript. The Workshop maps direct client statements to practices and objectives, preserves the speaker and timestamp, and applies useful working context without creating assessment conclusions.

          
Guardrail: meeting-derived statements may support owners, evidence claims, provider responsibilities, questions, notes, and Client Stated in Workshop validation basis. They do not automatically mark a practice or objective Met, Not Met, evidence sufficient, workbook validated, assessment-ready, or final.

        

        

          
Load transcript or meeting-context package

          
          

            
            
          

          
          

            
            
            
          

          
          
No transcript or meeting-context package selected.

          
DOCX and rich Teams recap files should be processed by DocConverter-L2G and exported as l2g_meeting_context_v1.json. Raw VTT/TXT/MD support is provided as a local fallback.

        

        

          
Pre-flight & mapping summary

          

          
Analyze a transcript to see mappings, owner statements, provider responsibilities, and conflicts.

          

            
            
            
          

          

            
            
          

        

        

          
Mapped statements and exceptions

          

            
            
          

          

        

        

          
Ownership and provider responsibility intelligence

          
This view combines explicit meeting statements with imported provider/scope context. Direct statements are applied as working values when safe; conflicting or ambiguous statements remain visible for advisor resolution.

          

        

      

    



    

      

        

          
Toolchain Contract, Coverage & Sync QA Stable handoff + merge controls

          
Validate the Workshop handoff before it is sent to L2G Builder/Merger. This page checks complete practice and objective coverage, source lineage, field-level provenance, package direction, workbook-write guardrails, and adjacent-tool compatibility.

          
Current sync position: Workshop v76 preserves the stable Workshop Handoff 1.7 and Workbook Merge 1.1 baseline. DocConverter-L2G v7.9.1 and L2G Scoper v3.11 remain the current validated upstream references pending their bounded Glean-directed releases or audit decisions. v72-v75 established the responsibility foundation, provider packs, and explicit reconciliation overlay. v76 makes that guidance usable one practice at a time during live advisory review while retaining the full matrix under Advanced / Bulk Review. Primary Workshop, workbook, SSP, scope, evidence-sufficiency, and assessment fields are never changed automatically. L2G Builder/Merger v3.6 and SSP v1.7 remain validated downstream/round-trip references.

        

        

          
Handoff and round-trip pre-flight

          

          
Run Sync & QA to validate the current workspace and handoff package.

Toolchain architecture & compatibility Operator view

          
This human-readable map shows which tool owns each decision stage, the current validated versions, and the stable package contracts. It is an operator aid only and does not replace package pre-flight.

          

          

        

        

          
Production freeze & runtime self-check v60 baseline

          
Verify the local application shell, stable package contracts, official practice/objective catalogs, offline restrictions, and recovery controls before using this file as a production baseline.

          

          
Run the runtime self-check after opening the file locally.

          

            
            
            
            
          

          

        

        

          
Stable handoff contract

          
The existing package names remain unchanged. Workshop Handoff 1.7 and Workbook Merge 1.1 are frozen as the v60 production baseline. Future additions must remain backward-compatible unless a separately versioned contract is deliberately introduced.

          

        

      

    


    

      

        

          
Import Scope Context / Scoper Return Advisor-controlled

          
Use this page for l2g_scope_context_v1.json from DocConverter-L2G and l2g_scope_return_package_v1.json from L2G Scoper. Imported scope context is draft-only support context for Workshop Review, Evidence Follow-up, Advisor Review, Detail Register, and later workbook handoff support.

          
Guardrail: scope context is not a final scope determination, not practice readiness, not evidence sufficiency, and not workbook validation. Scope-reducing claims, out-of-scope claims, provider inheritance, and prior MSP/provider evidence stay advisor-review-required.

        

        

          
Load scope package

          
Paste or upload the JSON package. The package is read locally in the browser. Nothing changes until the preview/diff is reviewed and checked in Import & Review.

          
          

            
            
            
          

          
          
No package selected yet.

        

        

          
Detection badges

          

          

          
No pre-flight run yet.

        

        

          
Scope context currently stored in this workshop

          
This stored context feeds live Workshop Review prompts, Advisor Review candidates, Detail Register candidates, and future advisory report sections. It remains draft-only.

          

            
            
            
            
          

          

        

        

          
Technology, service & provider profile Scoper v3.11 additive context

          
Scoper v3.11 can supply structured service, environment, enabled-module, access, evidence, and responsibility-source context. The profile remains collection-only and advisor-review-required. Import & Review stages suggested Workshop profile values rather than silently overwriting them.

          

          
No structured Scoper technology profile is currently loaded.

          

        

        

          
Candidate preview

          
The first rows below show what the package contains. Detailed rows are staged to the Import & Review preview/diff and, after apply, to the stored support context.

          

        

      

    


    

      

        

          
Trusted Import & Review Advisor-controlled

          
Use this page to bring DocConverter-L2G, L2G Scoper, workbook, CSV, or approved AI draft content into the workshop tool safely. The normal workflow is: load package, preview grouped changes, select recommended safe changes or manually check rows, then apply checked changes.

          
Important: imported content is draft intake context. The tool may trust routing metadata such as valid Capability_ID or Practice_ID, but it does not treat imported content as evidence sufficiency, practice readiness, or final CMMC status.

          
Workbook merge note: use the Workbook Loop page for l2g_workbook_merge_v1.json. The intake import center is for DocConverter-L2G / L2G Scoper draft packages.

        

        

          
Import workflow

          

            
1
Load package
Use the intake package box for DocConverter-L2G or L2G Scoper JSON. Legacy JSON, CSV, templates, and AI prompts are under Advanced.

            
2
Review changes
Use the summary, filters, and row checkboxes to decide what should land in the workshop state. Long narrative fields remain editable before applying.

            
3
Apply safely
Select recommended safe rows or manually check rows, then apply checked changes. Capability quick-apply never changes practice readiness or evidence sufficiency.

          

        

        

          
Package trust & exception queue
Review package identity, compatibility, low-confidence values, conflicts, invalid rows, and skipped content before applying changes.
No package staged

          
Waiting for a packageLoad a supported DocConverter-L2G or L2G Scoper package below. The queue updates automatically when preview data is staged.

          

          

          

            
            
            
            Selection does not apply data. Only Apply Checked Changes merges staged values.
          

        

        

          
Import center: load, preview, apply checked changes

          
Use the L2G Intake Package JSON path for DocConverter-L2G and L2G Scoper handoff. Paste is the most reliable local-file workflow; upload is also supported. CSV and legacy JSON imports are advanced troubleshooting paths.

          
Recommended handoff: DocConverter-L2G or L2G Scoper package → paste/drop the L2G package into the intake package box → validate/preview → use Select Recommended Safe Changes or check rows manually → Apply Checked Changes. Capability quick-apply is available when valid capability rows are present. Practice readiness and evidence sufficiency remain review-gated.

          

            

              
1. Load intake package

              
Preferred path for DocConverter-L2G and L2G Scoper handoff. Upload, drop, or paste l2g_intake_package_v1.json, l2g_scope_context_v1.json, l2g_scope_return_package_v1.json, or a legacy crm_intake_package_v1 package. Nothing is applied until changes are reviewed and checked below.

              

                Paste JSON or drop the L2G DocConverter-L2G / L2G Scoper package here
                Use the L2G intake package, DocConverter-L2G scope context package, or L2G Scoper return package. Legacy CRM packages are accepted temporarily and shown as legacy. The package is read locally and staged below; no rows are merged automatically.
              

              
              
              

                
                
                
              

              
No package selected yet. Paste L2G JSON is the most reliable path in local file:// use.

              
Draft only: DocConverter-L2G and L2G Scoper rows remain staged until reviewed and applied. Capability rows can be applied quickly when valid; practice readiness, sufficiency, and workbook-style statuses remain deliberate-review items.

            

            

              Advanced import options / troubleshooting
              
Use these only for legacy AI JSON, fallback CSV, templates, schema export, or troubleshooting. They are not the normal DocConverter-L2G / L2G Scoper workflow.

              

                

                  
Legacy JSON paste / upload

                  
Accepts a full workshop JSON state, a partial JSON object, or an object containing setup, documents, decisions, capabilities, and practices. Existing fields are merged and uncertain items can remain blank or Unknown.

                  
                  
                  

                    
                    
                    
                  

                  
No JSON file selected yet.

                

                

                  
CSV fallback

                  
Fallback/debug path only. Use a populated Section column with values: setup, document, decision, capability, or practice. Header-only/template CSVs will be rejected with a clear message.

                  
                  
                  

                    
                    
                    
                  

                  
CSV is fallback only. Prefer the intake package JSON path above.

                

                

                  
Templates, prompts, and schema

                  
Use these when an approved AI tool or spreadsheet workflow cannot produce valid package JSON.

                  

                    
                    
                    
                    
                    
                    
                    
                  

                

              

            

          

          

            
2. Review and apply staged changes

            
Imports are staged here first. Review the proposed changes, then either select recommended safe rows or manually check the rows you want. Applying checked changes is the only action that merges data into the workshop state.

            
Button logic: Select Recommended Safe Changes checks low-risk eligible rows but does not merge them. Apply Checked Changes merges only rows currently checked. This keeps quick import helpful while preserving advisor control.

            

            

              
              
              
              
              
              

                More actions / reports
                

                  
                  
                  
                  
                

              

            

            

              
              

                Practice draft controls
                

                  
                  
                

              

              Practice changes are draft-only and do not indicate readiness or evidence sufficiency.
            

            

            

            

              Import audit log / troubleshooting
              
Most recent import reports are saved with the workshop state for troubleshooting and defensibility.

              

            

          

          
Advanced CSV schema reference
          
CSV row typeRequired / useful headersUse case

            
setup / sowSection, Field, ValuePopulate orgName, envName, provider labels, target, SOW deliverables, timeline, evidence due date, provider dependencies, docs, and caveats.

            
documentSection, Document_Name, Type, Version, Date, Owner, Status, Review_Basis, Used_In_Workshop, Reason_Not_Used, Supports, NotesOne row per file received; strongest fallback for 20+ document packages.

            
decisionSection, Decision_Type, Topic, Statement, Source_Basis, Owner, Status, NotesCapture AI-found assumptions, dependencies, caveats, and follow-up questions.

            
capabilitySection, Capability_ID, Owner, Evidence_Maturity, Docs_Requested, NotesPrefill the high-level capability map before the workshop.

            
practiceSection, Practice_ID, Implementation_Owner, Evidence_Owner, Readiness, Evidence_Status, Confidence, Validation_Basis, NotesChunked practice updates by family, e.g., AC rows only, then AT rows.

          

          

        


        

          
Optional AI prompt reference

          
Use only with an approved AI environment when the converter package path is unavailable or a small manual assist is needed. Prefer DocConverter-L2G / L2G Scoper packages for normal workflow.

          

            Show copyable AI prompt
            

            
          

        

      

    



    

      

        

          
Workspace, history, and recovery Advisor-only

          
Use this advanced page to verify the current engagement identity, review imported package history, inspect unresolved conflicts, and manage local recovery. It does not change practice or objective conclusions.

          
Workspace safety: browser storage is convenient working memory, not the engagement record. Export Workshop JSON at meaningful checkpoints and before replacing a workspace.

          

        

        

          
Engagement identity

          

          

            
            
            
          

          
          
        

        

          
Display and accessibility

          

            
            
            
          

          

            Keyboard support
            
Use Tab and Shift+Tab to move through controls. Focused Practice Review continues to support Alt+Left and Alt+Right for previous/next practice when focus is outside a form field. The skip link appears when keyboard focus enters the page.

          

          

        

        

          
Package and workspace history
Combined view of DocConverter/Scoper imports, meeting-context sources, reviewed-workbook merges, Workshop state imports, and exports.

          

        

        

          
Conflict and exception queue
Review only items that could not be applied cleanly or that need an explicit advisor decision. Marking an item reviewed does not change its underlying source value.

          

          

        

        

          
Resume work

          

        

      

    


    

      

        

          
Sources, caveats, and intended use Advisor-only

          
This tool is intended to complement the existing readiness guide and workbooks. Practice content, evidence prompts, cadence notes, and SSP language are sourced from the uploaded Practice Frequency Matrix, which references official CMMC materials. This page is for advisor/source traceability, not client presentation.

          
    
            
  • DoD CIO CMMC Resources & Documentation: https://dodcio.defense.gov/CMMC/Resources-Documentation/
    • 
            
  • CMMC Level 2 Assessment Guide: https://dodcio.defense.gov/Portals/0/Documents/CMMC/AssessmentGuideL2v2.pdf
    • 
            
  • CMMC Level 2 Scoping Guide: https://dodcio.defense.gov/Portals/0/Documents/CMMC/ScopingGuideL2v2.pdf
    • 
            
  • CMMC Program Rule, 32 CFR Part 170: https://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2024/10/15/2024-22905/cybersecurity-maturity-model-certification-cmmc-program
    • 
            
  • DFARS CMMC Final Rule: https://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2025/09/10/2025-17359/defense-federal-acquisition-regulation-supplement-assessing-contractor-implementation-of
    • 
          

          
Important: Workshop entries are advisor workshop notes. Formal assessment status should be maintained in the Gap Analysis Workbook and supporting evidence repository.
